title: maianthemum racemosum subsp. racemosum
intro: Maianthemum racemosum subsp. racemosum, also known as False Lily-of-the-valley, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Asparagaceae family. The plant's roots and rhizomes are used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Maianthemum racemosum subsp. racemosum include its potential to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing its roots and rhizomes in hot water to create teas or tinctures, which can be used to support immune function and overall well-being.
